mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue, depressing equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate rising above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts growth indicates supply chain bottlenecks easing while consumer demand remains constrained, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds HOUST growth by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Forward returns show no significant negative skew when industrial production outpaces housing starts growth over a rolling 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle expansion, accelerating real GDP growth expands profit margins and drives positive equity returns, but this effect reverses when growth exceeds potential, triggering inflation fears and valuation resets.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5% annualized over two consecutive quarters, contradicting the positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaks above 200, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand and forward returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL remaining above 200 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with declining consumer credit balances indicates unsustainable debt-fueled consumption that will soon reverse, leading to a demand shock and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises more than 150k MoM while RSAFS falls more than 5% MoM
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where payroll growth exceeds 150k and consumer credit declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding capacity utilization and robust corporate output, which directly supports earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window despite consistent INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1800 units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, which precedes broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where HOUST drops below 1800, refuting the negative demand signal.
